LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER'S MOTION TO QUASH IN WHOLE OR IN PART DEFENDANT PG&E'S OVERBROAD RECORDS SUBPOENA IS GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY AS TO THE SUBPOENAS DIRECTED TO PLAINTIFF'S FORMER EMPLOYERS, AND GRANTED, IN PART AS TO THE MEDICAL SUBPOENAS. PG&E'S ARGUMENTS IN OPPOSITION LACK LEGAL AUTHORITY AND FACTUAL JUSTIFICATION. THE MEDICAL SUBPOENAS MUST BE LIMITED TO THE CONDITION AT ISSUE, THAT IS, MS. WALKER'S WORKPLACE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (BRITT V. SUP. COURT (1978) 20 CAL.3D 844, 864.)AS TO DR. MORGAN, THE SUBPOENA IS LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS RELATING TO HIS PLACING PLAINTIFF ON APPROVED MEDICAL LEAVE FROM FEBRUARY- DECEMBER 2010 AND DOCUMENTS RELATING TO EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS FROM 2003 TO PRESENT DATE. AS TO DR. ELLIS, THE SUBPOENA IS LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS RELATING TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF WORKPLACE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF, BUT NOT INCLUDING ATTACHMENT B (OR ANY REFERENCE TO ATTACHMENT B IN THE PROTECTIVE ORDER). IT CREATES CONFUSION TO HAVE THE ACKNOWLEDGMENT AND AGREEMENT RESTATE OR PARAPHRASE THE TERMS OF THE COURT ORDER TO WHICH THE SIGNATORY IS BOUND. THE PROTECTIVE ORDER WILL INCLUDE A PROVISION REQUIRING THE RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE CASE, INCLUDING ANY APPEAL. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MARY DUTRA, CSR# 9251, 415.398.1889.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANTS TO PRODUCE ALL NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE REQUESTS ARE OVERBROAD. FOR REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 103, ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF IS CORRECT THAT, ON A CASE BY CASE BASIS, EVIDENCE OF SIMILAR INCIDENTS CAN BE PROBATIVE OF AN EMPLOYER'S MOTIVE OR INTENT REGARDING AN INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFF. (JOHNSON V. UNITED CEREBRAL PALSY/SPASTIC CHILDREN'S FOUND. OF LOS ANGELES & VENTURA COUNTIES (2009)173 CAL.APP.4TH 740, 767.) HOWEVER, IN THIS CASE, IT DOES NOT FOLLOW THAT DEFENDANT MUST PRODUCE ALL EVIDENCE COMPANY-WIDE. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE THAT IS HAS SEARCHED FOR ANY JUDGMENTS FROM PLAINTIFF'S GEOGRAPHIC REGION FOR THE TIME PERIOD FROM 2003 TO 2011 IS SUFFICIENT. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $ 2,000.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT PG&E AND AGAINST PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO EMPLOYMENT FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS IS GRANTED, IN PART. WITHIN 10 DAYS, DEFENDANT PG&E SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 200.4(A), 201.1(C)-(D), 201.3(D), 201.4(E)-(F), 208.1(C)-(D) 208.1(E)-(F). 208.2(D), 215.1 AND 215.2. FOR FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 208.2(D), 215.1, AND 215.2, DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE RESPONSES WOULD REVEAL ANY ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT. FOR FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 200.4(A), 201.1(D), 201.3(D) AND 201.4(F), DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES THAT OMIT THE QUALIFIER "INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO" SO AS TO INDICATE THAT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ARE CONTAINED WITHIN THE RESPONSE. FOR FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS.208.2(D), 215.1 AND 215.2, THE PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED THAT WITNESSES WHO ARE EMPLOYED BY DEFENDANT MAY BE SUBPOENAED FOR DEPOSITION OR TRIAL THROUGH DEFENDANT. FOR THIRD PARTY WITNESSES, DEFENDANT SHALL IDENTIFY THOSE WITNESSES WHO WERE INVOLVED IN THE INVESTIGATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS OR MAY HAVE INFORMATION RELATED TO THEM, OR HOW DEFENDANT DEALT WITH THEM. Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attorneys PHIL HOROWITZ and CHRISTOPHER BANKS of the Law Offices of Phil Horowitz, Tel No. (415) 391-0111 appearing for the Plaintiff Julie Walker;
Attorneys GARY T. LAFAYETTE and REBECCA K. KIMURA of Lafayette & Kumagai, LLP, Tel No. (415) 357-4600 appearing for Defendants Danny Buist and Pacific Gas & Electric Company.

RULING - Matter presented by counsel as to the proposed form of order only. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion To Compel Defendants' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Nos. 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, And 125 is granted in part and denied in part. Specifically, as to Requests 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, and 125, Defendant is ordered to serve verified supplemental responses and produce documents no later than January 30, 2015. If defendant has no further documents, then the response should so state. Further, as to Request 111, Defendants are ordered to produce all responsive documents for copying at the PG&E Chico office no later than January 30, 2015. These responsive documents are to be produced subject to the protective order and are only to be used for the purposes of the present litigation. All copies of these documents are to be returned to Defendant PG&E within 90 days of the conclusion of this litigation and when all appeals have been exhausted. As to Request 118, Defendants are ordered to provide, subject to the protective order, information sufficient to show whether each employee who was the subject of the "employee performance records" produced by Defendants, Bates Numbered DEF005205-5211, was (1) male or female, and (2) 40 years of age or older. Finally, as to Request 124, Defendants are ordered to unseal all responsive documents and produce them to Plaintiff subject to the protective order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR #12948 (Tel No. 415-810-7170) (302/EHG)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attorneys PHIL HOROWITZ and CHRISTOPHER BANKS of the Law Offices of Phil Horowitz, Tel No. (415) 391-0111 appearing for the Plaintiff Julie Walker;
Attorneys GARY T. LAFAYETTE and REBECCA K. KIMURA of Lafayette & Kumagai, LLP, Tel No. (415) 357-4600 appearing for Defendants Danny Buist and Pacific Gas & Electric Company.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DANNY BUIST AND PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Stay Depositions And To Quash Plaintiff's Untimely Deposition Notices And Deposition Subpoenas is denied. Motion to Stay is denied as there is no reason to stay the depositions. Motion to Quash Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records is denied as the documents requested are relevant to the litigation and FRASCO did not contest the subpoena. Motions to Quash Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoenas for Personal Appearance to Dale Ulsh and Jim Faulbaum are granted due to Plaintiff's failure to serve an accompanying "notice to consumer or employee and objection" form per CCP 1985.6. However, if Plaintiff properly re-issues and serves deposition subpoenas for personal appearance and an accompanying notices to consumer or employee and objection to Ulsh and Faulbaum, then Defendants shall not have standing to challenge the subpoena as this Motion is denied solely on procedural grounds and the deposition notice is relevant on the merits. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR #12948 (Tel No. 415-810-7170) (302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Deft'S To Produce All Non-Privileged Documents Responsive To Pltf'S First Req. For Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER BANKS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF PHIL HOROWITZ, TEL. NO. 391-0111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. REBECCA K. KIMURA OF LAFAYETTE & KUMAGAI, TEL. NO. 357-4600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion For Order Compelling Defendants To Produce All Non-Privileged Documents Responsive To Plaintiff's First Request For Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Sanctions is Denied. The requests are overbroad. For Request for Production no. 103, although Plaintiff is correct that, on a case by case basis, evidence of similar incidents can be probative of an employer's motive or intent regarding an individual plaintiff. (Johnson v. United Cerebral Palsy/Spastic Children's Found. of Los Angeles & Ventura Counties (2009)173 Cal.App.4th 740, 767.) However, in this case, it does not follow that Defendant must produce all evidence company-wide. Defendant's response that is has searched for any judgments from Plaintiff's geographic region for the time period from 2003 to 2011 is sufficient. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,000.00 are awarded in favor of Defendant PG&E and against Plaintiff Julie Walker to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/EHG

Plaintiff Julie Walker'S Motion To Quash In Whole Or In Part Deft Pg&E'S Overbroad Records Subpoena
Off Calendar Jul-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRIS BANKS, ESQ., 415.391.0111, FOR PLAINTIFF JOULE WALKER.
REBECCA KIMURA, ESQ., 415.357.4600, FOR DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT WITHDRAWS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Julie Walker'S Motion To Quash In Whole Or In Part DEFENDANT Pg&E'S Overbroad Records Subpoena IS Granted in its entirety as to the subpoenas directed to Plaintiff's former employers, and granted, in part as to the medical subpoenas. PG&E's arguments in opposition lack legal authority and factual justification. The medical subpoenas must be limited to the condition at issue, that is, Ms. Walker's workplace emotional distress. (Britt v. Sup. Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.)AS TO DR. MORGAN, THE SUBPOENA IS LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS RELATING TO HIS PLACING PLAINTIFF ON APPROVED MEDICAL LEAVE FROM FEBRUARY- DECEMBER 2010 AND DOCUMENTS RELATING TO EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS FROM 2003 TO PRESENT DATE. AS TO DR. ELLIS, THE SUBPOENA IS LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS RELATING TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF WORKPLACE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. The Court will sign the proposed protective order submitted by Plaintiff, BUT NOT INCLUDING Attachment B (or any reference to attachment B in the protective order). It creates confusion to have the acknowledgment and agreement restate or paraphrase the terms of the Court order to which the signatory is bound. THE PROTECTIVE ORDER WILL INCLUDE A PROVISION REQUIRING THE RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE CASE, INCLUDING ANY APPEAL. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: MARY DUTRA, CSR# 9251, 415.398.1889.
